skills.sampleSkill:
---
name: my-skill
description: One sentence describing when this skill should be activated.
license: MIT
---

# Skill Content

System instructions for the Agent — style guides, constraints, workflow templates, etc.
The content of SKILL.md is automatically prepended to the agent's system prompt at runtime.

## Suggested Sections

- **When to activate**: A brief description of trigger scenarios.
- **Style/Rules**: Two to three hard constraints.
- **Self-check**: Items the Agent should verify before responding.

wechat.intro: Bind a personal WeChat by scanning a QR with the phone you want to use. Once bound, every message routes to the agent. No public IP needed.
steps: Save the channel (just give it a name + pick a default agent)
Click 'Scan to log in' on the channel card
Scan the QR with the WeChat you want to bind and confirm on your phone
The badge turns green 'Connected' — you're done

feishu.intro: Create a custom app on Feishu Open Platform, paste App ID + App Secret here. No public URL required.
steps: Open Feishu Open Platform, create a custom app and enable the Bot capability
Copy App ID and App Secret from 'Credentials & Basic Info'
Paste them into the two inputs below
Save — once the badge turns green you can @ the bot in Feishu

lark.intro: Create a custom app on Lark Developer, copy App ID + App Secret. No public URL required.
steps: Open Lark Developer, create a Custom App and enable the bot capability
Copy App ID and App Secret from 'Credentials & Basic Info'
Paste them into the inputs below
Save — when the badge turns green you can chat from Lark

dingtalk.intro: Create a robot app on DingTalk Open Platform and copy ClientID / ClientSecret. No public URL required.
steps: DingTalk Open Platform → create an in-house Robot app
Copy ClientID and ClientSecret from 'Credentials & Basic Info'
Paste them below
Save — add the robot to a group to start chatting

wecom.intro: Create an AIBot in WeCom admin, copy bot ID and bot secret. No public URL required.
steps: WeCom admin → Workspace → AIBot → Create
Copy the bot ID and bot secret
Paste them into the two inputs below
Save — add the robot to a group to start chatting

discord.intro: Create a Bot on Discord Developer Portal, copy Bot Token. No public URL required.
steps: Discord Developer Portal → new Application → add a Bot
Enable 'Message Content Intent' on the Bot page
Copy the Bot Token, paste it below
Use the OAuth2 invite URL to add the bot to your server
Save — @mention the bot or DM it to trigger replies

qq.intro: Create a robot on Tencent QQ Open Platform, copy AppID / AppSecret. No public URL required.
steps: QQ Open Platform → create a robot, wait for approval
Pick WebSocket as the message-receiving transport
Copy AppID and AppSecret, paste them below
Save — @mention the bot in QQ to chat

slack.intro: Slack needs a public callback URL. After saving below, paste the channel's Webhook URL back into Slack admin.
steps: Slack admin → create an App, enable Event Subscriptions
Copy Bot Token and Signing Secret into the inputs below
Save the channel and paste its Webhook URL back as Slack's Request URL
Slack sends url_verification — SenHarness ACKs automatically

teams.intro: Teams needs a public callback URL. Paste the signing secret here, then put the Webhook URL into Teams admin.
steps: Teams channel → add an Outgoing Webhook
Copy the signing secret into the input below
Save the channel, paste its Webhook URL back into Teams admin
Optional: add an Incoming Webhook URL for outbound replies

telegram.intro: Telegram uses bot webhook — needs public IP. Save below, then call setWebhook once.
steps: Create a Bot via BotFather, copy the Bot Token
Paste the Bot Token below; optionally set a Secret Token
Save the channel
Call Telegram Bot API setWebhook with url = the Webhook URL on the card

webhook.intro: Generic inbound: any system can POST JSON to the URL below to trigger an agent.
steps: Save the channel
Wire the Webhook URL into the system you want to integrate
POST JSON with the user message in `text` and optional thread id in `thread`

setupHint.slack: Slack admin → Event Subscriptions → Enable → Request URL = the URL above. Subscribe to 'message.channels' / 'app_mention'. Slack sends url_verification; we auto-ACK.
setupHint.feishu: Stream mode (recommended): Create a custom app in Feishu, paste App ID + App Secret below. Long-connection event mode needs no public URL. Webhook mode: put the URL above into Event Subscription Request URL.
setupHint.lark: Stream mode (recommended): Create a Lark app, paste App ID + App Secret below — long connection mode means no public URL. Webhook mode: paste the URL above into Request URL.
setupHint.discord: Stream mode (recommended): Discord Developer Portal → Bot → enable Message Content Intent. Paste the bot token below to dial Gateway directly. Webhook mode: Interactions Endpoint URL = the URL above.
setupHint.teams: Teams channel → Add Outgoing Webhook → paste the signing secret into signing_secret; optionally add an Incoming Webhook URL for replies.
setupHint.telegram: Call Telegram Bot API setWebhook, url = the URL above; pass secret_token so the bot side can authenticate inbound calls.
setupHint.wecom: Stream mode (recommended): WeCom admin → AIBot → create a robot. Paste bot_id + bot_secret below — outbound WebSocket, no public URL needed. Webhook mode: self-built app callback URL = the URL above; SenHarness handles VerifyURL automatically.
setupHint.wechat: iLink Bot flow: save the channel, then click 'Scan to log in' on the card. Scan the QR with your bound WeChat and confirm on your phone — the card turns green when connected. If the token expires (re-scan needed), the card turns red. No public URL required.
setupHint.dingtalk: Stream mode (recommended): DingTalk Open Platform → app credentials → paste ClientID / ClientSecret into client_id / client_secret. No public URL needed. Webhook mode: copy the custom-robot webhook into webhook_url and the SEC-prefixed sign secret into sign_secret.
setupHint.qq: Stream mode (recommended): QQ Bot Open Platform → choose WebSocket transport. Paste App ID / App Secret below. Webhook mode: paste the URL above into the callback field; we auto-respond to the op=13 signature handshake.
setupHint.webhook: Any system can POST JSON here; put the user message in `text` and an optional thread id in `thread`.

mcp.pageDescription: Register stdio / SSE / streamable-HTTP MCP servers, hand them OAuth credentials when needed, and probe their tool catalogues before binding them to a toolbox.
mcp.formDescription: stdio spawns a local process; SSE / streamable-HTTP open the modern HTTP-with-streaming transports. OAuth runs the dance once and stores tokens in the workspace vault.
mcp.commandHint: First word is the binary; further words become positional args.
mcp.argsHint: One arg per line — appended after the command.
mcp.bearerTokenHint: Sent as Authorization: Bearer '<token>'. Stored as-is on the row.
mcp.bearerRefHint: $'{vault://workspace/<key>}' — used instead of the inline token when set.
mcp.oauthSectionDescription: We POST to the token URL once and store the access + refresh token in the workspace vault. Tokens are never written to audit metadata.
mcp.clientSecretHint: Plaintext — rotated into the workspace vault on save and dropped from the row.
mcp.clientSecretRefHint: $'{vault://workspace/<key>}' — long-term shape; bypasses the one-time plaintext path.
mcp.scopesHint: Space-separated. Empty = no scope parameter sent.
mcp.refreshGraceHint: Refresh ahead of the IdP-reported expiry by this many seconds.
mcp.keepaliveSecondsHint: How often the client pings the server; defaults to 30 s.
mcp.requestTimeoutSecondsHint: Hard cap on call_tool — defaults to 300 s, capped at 1800 s.
mcp.maxConcurrentHint: Per-server semaphore. Calls past this wait in FIFO order; defaults to 4.

validation.slugFormat: Slug must start with a letter and contain only letters / digits / dashes / underscores (2–64 chars).

validation.missingOAuthTokenUrl: Token URL is required when OAuth is selected.
validation.missingOAuthSecret: Provide either a one-time client secret or a vault reference.
